organizationTypes.title: Core Product Engineering
organizationTypes.description: Translating ambiguous market requirements into shipped, stable, and highly performant code.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Release the new multi-tenant billing microservice to production by Q3.

organizationTypes.title: Site Reliability & Infrastructure
organizationTypes.description: Maintaining high availability and scaling infrastructure to meet user demand without manual intervention.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Migrate the primary database cluster to a multi-region active-active setup.

organizationTypes.title: Go-to-Market & Enterprise Sales
organizationTypes.description: Identifying targets, qualifying leads, and closing high-value software contracts.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Close $500k in new enterprise annual recurring revenue this quarter.

organizationTypes.title: Platform Engineering
organizationTypes.description: Product teams reinventing infrastructure badly; slow builds, flaky deploys, inconsistent tooling.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Cut median CI time to under 8 minutes and give every pod one-command preview environments.

organizationTypes.title: Security Operations
organizationTypes.description: Finding vulnerabilities before attackers do, and responding when they find them first.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Complete the quarterly threat review of the payments path and close all criticals.

organizationTypes.title: Data & Analytics
organizationTypes.description: Decisions being made on vibes because the data is scattered, stale, or wrong.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Stand up the retention dashboard and tell me whether January's pricing change actually worked.

organizationTypes.title: Applied ML
organizationTypes.description: Shipping models that are actually better than the heuristic they replace, with evidence.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Replace the rules-based fraud filter with a model at equal recall and half the false-positive rate.

organizationTypes.title: Product Design Studio
organizationTypes.description: Building the wrong thing beautifully, or the right thing unusably.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Redesign onboarding so activation hits 40%, with research to prove it.

organizationTypes.title: Growth Marketing
organizationTypes.description: Nobody knows the product exists; or they know and don't convert.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Launch the Q3 content campaign and grow organic signups 25%.

organizationTypes.title: Customer Support Center
organizationTypes.description: High ticket volume, wildly varying difficulty, and institutional amnesia about past fixes.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Hold first-response under 2 hours this quarter while cutting repeat-issue tickets 30%.

organizationTypes.title: High-Volume Franchise Operation
organizationTypes.description: Executing high-throughput, standardized physical service delivery within strict time and safety tolerances.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Serve a 200-car lunch rush while maintaining a sub-3-minute average ticket time.

organizationTypes.title: General Contracting & Construction
organizationTypes.description: Coordinating physical materials, specialized labor, and strict safety codes into a durable structure.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Build a custom 8x4 cedar lumber planter garden and wire the adjacent exterior electrical outlets.

organizationTypes.title: E-commerce Fulfillment
organizationTypes.description: Orders in, correct boxes out, at volume, with inventory that never lies.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Clear the Black Friday backlog within 48 hours at under 0.5% mis-pick rate.

organizationTypes.title: Outpatient Medical Clinic
organizationTypes.description: Patient flow, care quality, and documentation under regulatory constraint.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Run today's schedule of 40 appointments with complete intake notes and zero missed follow-ups.

organizationTypes.title: Event Production
organizationTypes.description: A hard deadline that cannot slip, dozens of vendors, and a day-of execution window measured in minutes.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Produce the 300-person launch conference on Oct 12, from venue to recap video.

organizationTypes.title: Academic Research Lab
organizationTypes.description: Expanding the boundary of human knowledge through rigorous testing, data modeling, and peer-reviewed publication.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Draft and publish a paper solving the atmospheric carbon-capture efficiency drop.

organizationTypes.title: Grassroots Community Organization
organizationTypes.description: Mobilizing local participants, securing resources, and synchronizing outdoor community events for a shared purpose.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Design and execute a 45-minute synchronized community fitness workout across a local high school football field.

organizationTypes.title: The Newsroom
organizationTypes.description: Publishing stories fast enough to matter and verified enough to defend.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Investigate the city procurement records and publish by Friday if the story holds.

organizationTypes.title: Curriculum Studio
organizationTypes.description: Turning expertise into teachable, assessable learning material.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Build the 6-week 'Intro to Data Analysis' course: outline, twelve modules, assessments.

organizationTypes.title: Nonprofit Development Office
organizationTypes.description: Funding a mission from grants, donors, and volunteers, with radical accountability for where money goes.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Raise $250k this fiscal year across grants and the spring donor campaign.

organizationTypes.title: Marketing / Creative Agency
organizationTypes.description: Delivering creative work that satisfies a client who changes their mind, on retainer economics.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Deliver the full rebrand package for Client X: identity, site copy, launch assets.

organizationTypes.title: Management Consultancy
organizationTypes.description: Answering a high-stakes strategic question with rigor, on a deadline, for someone else.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Should Client Y enter the Brazilian market? Recommendation deck in six weeks.

organizationTypes.title: Law Practice
organizationTypes.description: Legal throughput — contracts, redlines, filings — where errors are expensive and everything needs review.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Clear the M&A due-diligence document room by the 15th, flagging every change-of-control clause.

organizationTypes.title: Talent Acquisition
organizationTypes.description: Filling open roles with signal, not luck, at pipeline scale.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Fill the three open senior engineering roles this quarter.

organizationTypes.title: Finance Back Office
organizationTypes.description: Books that close on time and survive audit.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Close March by the 5th business day with all reconciliations reviewed.

organizationTypes.title: Legal & Compliance Desk
organizationTypes.description: Contract review and regulatory adherence as an internal service.
organizationTypes.exampleIntent: Every inbound NDA triaged within 24 hours; quarterly compliance audit complete by the 20th.

roles.title: Chief Executive
roles.purpose: Org root; owns the standing intent and delegates everything else

responsibilities.duty: Decompose standing intent into child Assignments

roles.title: General Manager
roles.purpose: Runs a self-contained business unit against a P&L

roles.title: Team Lead
roles.purpose: First-line manager of a small delivery team

roles.title: Chief of Staff
roles.purpose: Aggregates upward reporting; runs the operating cadence

roles.title: Coordinator
roles.purpose: General-purpose logistics and scheduling under any manager

roles.title: Engineering Lead
roles.purpose: Technical decomposition and code quality for a pod

roles.title: SRE Lead
roles.purpose: Reliability standards and infra spend oversight

roles.title: Solutions Engineer
roles.purpose: Technical depth in the sales cycle

roles.title: Recruiter
roles.purpose: Own the hiring pipeline for open roles

roles.title: Sourcer
roles.purpose: Fill the top of the hiring funnel

roles.title: Controller
roles.purpose: Accurate books and the monthly close

roles.title: Line Cook (Station)
roles.purpose: Execute one station to spec and tempo

roles.title: General Contractor
roles.purpose: Sequence trades, hold the master budget

formations.title: Product Engineering Pod
formations.purpose: Ship product features end-to-end with built-in verification.
formations.artifactFlow: brief → engineers produce PullRequest → DependencyGate opens QA → TestReport → lead accepts both → publishes upward

formations.title: Incident Response Squad
formations.purpose: Restore service fast, then convert the pain into prevention.
formations.artifactFlow: alert → responder TriageNote → recovery RecoveryAttestation → postmortem IncidentPostmortem → tech-writer RunbookDoc

formations.title: Platform Pod
formations.purpose: Internal developer platform — CI/CD, infra, paved roads.
formations.artifactFlow: InfraSpec → implementation PullRequest → security SecurityReport gate → release

formations.title: Design Studio Cell
formations.purpose: Turn product questions into validated, buildable designs.
formations.artifactFlow: ResearchPlan → ResearchReport → DependencyGate → DesignSpec + CopyDoc → lead review → publish to engineering

formations.title: Data Insights Cell
formations.purpose: Answer questions with data; keep the data trustworthy enough to ask.
formations.artifactFlow: question brief → engineer PullRequest (pipeline) → analyst Dashboard/AnalysisReport → scientist FindingsReport for the hard questions

formations.title: ML Delivery Pod
formations.purpose: Train, evaluate, and ship models with evidence.
formations.artifactFlow: ExperimentDesign → DataQualityReport → ModelCard → EvalReport (QA-style gate) → deployment PR

formations.title: Sales Pod
formations.purpose: Take a territory from cold to closed.
formations.artifactFlow: SDR QualifiedLead → DependencyGate → AE Proposal → SignedContract; SE feeds QuestionnaireResponse/DemoAttestation into the AE's stages

formations.title: Support Tier
formations.purpose: Resolve customer issues at the cheapest competent level, and never solve the same problem twice.
formations.artifactFlow: ticket → agent ResolutionAttestation, or EscalationPackage → engineer DiagnosisReport → KB writer KBArticle

formations.title: Content Machine
formations.purpose: Produce, polish, distribute, and measure content on a cadence.
formations.artifactFlow: CampaignBrief → writer ContentPiece → editor EditedDraft (quality gate) → PublishAttestation → analyst EngagementReport

formations.title: Recruiting Loop
formations.purpose: Fill an open role from sourcing to signed offer.
formations.artifactFlow: CandidateList → ScreenNote → coordinator BookingConfirmation + FeedbackPacket → recruiter OfferPacket

formations.title: Research Cell
formations.artifactFlow: ResearchBrief → parallel LitReview + Dataset/DataModel → DependencyGate → Manuscript → PI review

formations.title: Franchise Shift
formations.artifactFlow: OrderTicket fans out to stations in parallel → StationAttestations → expeditor OrderCompleteAttestation

formations.title: Build Crew
formations.purpose: Sequence physical trades where dependencies are physics, not preference.
formations.artifactFlow: WorkSchedule → carpenter BuildAttestation → DependencyGate → electrician WiringAttestation → inspector InspectionReport (ApprovalGate) → GC acceptance

formations.title: Newsdesk
formations.purpose: Investigate and publish stories that survive scrutiny.
formations.artifactFlow: StoryBrief → reporter StoryDraft → parallel FactCheckReport + EditedDraft → editor-in-chief ApprovalGate → publish

formations.title: Event Crew
formations.purpose: Take an event from concept through day-of execution to follow-up.
formations.artifactFlow: EventRunSheet → coordinator BookingConfirmations (permits/venue behind ApprovalGates) → day-of ShowAttestation + RecordingFile → writer post-event ContentPiece

formations.title: Fundraising Office
formations.artifactFlow: FundraisingPlan → parallel GrantProposals + donor MeetingAttestations + VolunteerRoster → quarterly StatusReport rollup
